findings.title: Migrate from Deprecated Kafka Version
findings.description: Migrate the core message broker from Kafka 2.8 to Kafka 3.7+ to address end-of-life concerns and known vulnerabilities.
assumptions: Kafka 3.7+ is stable and compatible with existing services.

evidence.excerpt: Core message broker runs Kafka 2.8 which reaches end-of-life in Q1 2026.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current Kafka version is 2.8 WHEN migration to 3.7+ is initiated THEN all services should function without errors and performance should be stable.

findings.title: Eliminate Vendor Lock-in on Proprietary ETL SDK
findings.description: Evaluate and plan migration from VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK to open-source alternatives to mitigate financial risks.
assumptions: Open-source alternatives can meet current functionality.

evidence.excerpt: The data transformation layer relies on VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK.

recommendations: Conduct a feasibility study on alternative ETL solutions.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current ETL SDK is VendorX's WHEN alternatives are evaluated THEN a report should be generated outlining at least three viable options.

findings.title: Upgrade Python for Pipeline Workers
findings.description: Upgrade all pipeline worker services from Python 3.9 to Python 3.12 to address end-of-life security concerns.
assumptions: All dependencies will be compatible with Python 3.12.

evidence.excerpt: Pipeline worker services run Python 3.9 which reaches end-of-life October 2025.

recommendations: Identify and resolve compatibility issues with dependencies.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current Python version is 3.9 WHEN the upgrade to 3.12 is completed THEN all pipeline worker services should operate without errors.

findings.title: Implement Multi-Region Caching Solution
findings.description: Migrate from a single-region Redis cluster to a multi-region caching solution to reduce latency for global users.
assumptions: Multi-region caching will effectively reduce latency.

evidence.excerpt: The entire caching layer is a single Redis 6 cluster in us-east-1.

recommendations: Evaluate Redis 7+ and alternative caching solutions.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current caching solution is single-region WHEN the multi-region solution is implemented THEN latency for users outside the US should be reduced to under 100ms.

findings.title: Create Dependency Health Dashboard
findings.description: Develop a centralized dashboard for monitoring dependency health, including EOL dates and known CVEs.
assumptions: A dashboard will improve proactive management of dependencies.

evidence.excerpt: There is no centralized view of dependency health across the platform.

recommendations: Integrate with GitHub Dependabot and Snyk for alerts.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the dashboard is developed WHEN accessed THEN it should display all dependencies with their versions, EOL dates, and known CVEs.

findings.title: Implement Automated CVE Alerting
findings.description: Integrate automated alerting for critical CVEs to improve response times for vulnerabilities.
assumptions: Automated alerts will lead to faster resolution of CVEs.

evidence.excerpt: Build a dashboard showing all runtime dependencies with versions, EOL dates, known CVEs.

recommendations: Test the alerting system for effectiveness.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the alerting system is implemented WHEN a critical CVE is identified THEN an alert should be sent to the responsible team within 5 minutes.

findings.title: Monitor Events Processed Per Day
findings.description: Establish a monitoring system for the North Star metric: Events Processed Per Day to track platform performance.
assumptions: Monitoring this metric will provide insights into platform performance.

evidence.excerpt: This metric represents the total number of events processed by the DataPipe platform daily.

recommendations: Create dashboards to visualize this metric.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN events are processed THEN the total should be accurately reflected in the dashboard.

findings.title: Track Services on EOL Dependencies
findings.description: Implement a tracking system for services relying on end-of-life dependencies to assess platform risk.
assumptions: Tracking EOL dependencies will help prioritize migration efforts.

evidence.excerpt: This metric tracks the number of services relying on end-of-life dependencies.

recommendations: Integrate tracking into the dependency health dashboard.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the tracking system is implemented WHEN a service is identified as relying on EOL dependencies THEN it should be logged and reported.

findings.title: Count Critical CVEs Open
findings.description: Implement a system to count and report the number of critical CVEs that are currently open.
assumptions: Counting CVEs will help prioritize security efforts.

evidence.excerpt: This metric counts the number of critical Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that are currently open.

recommendations: Integrate this count into the dependency health dashboard.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the counting system is in place WHEN a critical CVE is identified THEN it should be reflected in the report within 24 hours.

findings.title: Experiment with CVE Resolution Time
findings.description: Conduct an experiment to measure the impact of implementing automated CVE alerting on the resolution time for critical vulnerabilities.
assumptions: Automated alerting will reduce resolution time.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct an experiment to measure the impact of implementing automated CVE alerting on the resolution time for critical vulnerabilities.

recommendations: Design the experiment and define success metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the experiment is conducted WHEN automated CVE alerting is implemented THEN the average resolution time for critical CVEs should be reduced by at least 30%.

summary: The analysis resulted in a comprehensive set of requirements addressing critical migration needs, vendor lock-in risks, and security concerns. Each requirement is structured with clear acceptance criteria and prioritization to guide the development process effectively.
